Methodological limitations were common, including inadequate events-per-variable ratios, a near-absence of robust external validation (only 5 of 40 studies), inconsistent outcome definitions, and incomplete reporting of model specifications. Furthermore, over a quarter (27.5%) of models omitted calibration assessment, and decision-curve analysis was rarely performed, limiting insights into clinical utility.ConclusionML-based prediction models demonstrate strong potential for risk stratification in AD across multiple clinically relevant outcomes.
